status_notes: Initial research from Google Cloud Security Command Center documentation, 2026-04-04. Pass 1 (service docs). Needs Pass 2 (API), Pass 3 (cross-reference), and Pass 5 (human review with live GCP environment). Note: brief summary also exists in logging.json.
notes: Centralized security and risk management. Standard tier (free) and Premium/Enterprise tiers. Vulnerability scanning, threat detection, compliance monitoring.

data.description: Automated vulnerability detection that scans Google Cloud resources for misconfigurations. Standard tier covers basic checks: public IPs, open firewall rules, unencrypted storage, misconfigured IAM. Premium tier adds 100+ additional detectors covering CIS, PCI-DSS, NIST, and ISO benchmarks. Findings are generated for each detected misconfiguration with severity, resource details, and remediation guidance.

options.description: Basic misconfiguration detection. Covers: public IP addresses, open firewall rules, public buckets, MFA enforcement, logging configuration.
100+ additional detectors. Full CIS Google Cloud Foundations Benchmark coverage, PCI-DSS, NIST 800-53, ISO 27001 mappings.

default.value: Standard tier available at no cost
default.note: Standard tier detectors run automatically when SCC is activated. Premium detectors require Premium or Enterprise tier.

data.vendor_recommendation: Activate SCC at the organization level for comprehensive coverage. Use Premium tier for production environments. Review and remediate high-severity findings regularly. Export findings to BigQuery for trend analysis.

audit.console: Cloud Console → Security → Security Command Center → Findings → Filter by source: Security Health Analytics

configure.console: Cloud Console → Security → Security Command Center → Settings → Security Health Analytics
configure.note: Security Health Analytics is configured at the organization level. Individual detector modules can be enabled or disabled in Premium tier.

match_nodes.description: Log-based threat detection from audit and network logs

data.description: Log-based threat detection that monitors Cloud Audit Logs, VPC Flow Logs, and DNS logs for suspicious activity. Detects cryptocurrency mining, malware, brute force attacks, data exfiltration, IAM anomalies, and other threats. Uses built-in detection rules and threat intelligence. Requires Premium or Enterprise tier.

options.description: DNS requests to known mining pools, suspicious compute usage patterns
Known malicious domains, suspicious network connections, bad IP reputation
SSH brute force, repeated failed authentication attempts
Unusual data transfer patterns, large volume downloads
Unusual IAM grants, service account key creation, policy changes from suspicious sources
Addition of SSH keys, startup scripts, or other persistence mechanisms

default.note: Requires SCC Premium or Enterprise tier at the organization level.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable Event Threat Detection at the organization level. Ensure Cloud Audit Logs (Admin Activity and Data Access) are enabled for comprehensive coverage. Route findings to your SIEM for correlation.

audit.console: Cloud Console → Security → Security Command Center → Findings → Filter by source: Event Threat Detection

configure.console: Cloud Console → Security → Security Command Center → Settings → Event Threat Detection
configure.note: Event Threat Detection is enabled by default when Premium tier is activated. Custom detection modules can be configured.

match_nodes.description: Runtime threat detection for GKE clusters

data.description: Runtime threat detection for Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) clusters. Detects suspicious binary execution, reverse shells, suspicious library loading, and malicious scripts at the container runtime level. Uses a lightweight DaemonSet agent deployed to GKE nodes. Requires Premium or Enterprise tier.

options.description: Execution of known malicious binaries or binaries from unexpected locations
Network connections initiated from a container that redirect stdin/stdout to a remote socket
Loading of libraries that indicate exploitation attempts or malicious activity
Execution of known malicious scripts or scripting patterns associated with attacks

default.note: Requires GKE clusters with Container Threat Detection agent deployed.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable Container Threat Detection for all production GKE clusters. Ensure the agent is deployed to all node pools. Review findings promptly as runtime detections indicate active threats.

audit.console: Cloud Console → Security → Security Command Center → Findings → Filter by source: Container Threat Detection

configure.console: Cloud Console → Security → Security Command Center → Settings → Container Threat Detection
configure.note: Container Threat Detection requires the Container Threat Detection agent to be deployed to GKE clusters. This is managed automatically when enabled.

match_nodes.description: Automated vulnerability scanning for web applications

data.description: Automated vulnerability scanning for web applications running on App Engine, GKE, and Compute Engine. Scans for common web vulnerabilities including XSS, SQL injection, mixed content, outdated libraries, and insecure configuration. Standard tier provides manual-only scanning. Premium tier adds managed scanning (automatic, scheduled).

options.description: Manual scans only. User-initiated. Basic vulnerability detection.
Managed scans (automatic). Scheduled scanning. Additional vulnerability detectors.

data.vendor_recommendation: Configure scans for all internet-facing web applications. Use managed scans (Premium) for continuous assessment. Remediate high and medium findings before production deployment.

audit.console: Cloud Console → Security → Web Security Scanner

match_nodes.description: OS vulnerability scanning for Compute Engine VMs

data.description: OS vulnerability scanning for Compute Engine virtual machines. Uses the OS Config agent to inventory installed packages and detect known vulnerabilities (CVEs). Requires VM Manager (OS Config) to be enabled. Findings are surfaced in Security Command Center with CVE details and remediation guidance.

default.note: OS Config agent must be installed and VM Manager enabled at the project level.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable VM Manager for all projects with Compute Engine instances. Ensure the OS Config agent is installed on all VMs. Review and patch high-severity vulnerabilities promptly.

audit.console: Cloud Console → Security → Security Command Center → Findings → Filter by category: OS_VULNERABILITY

configure.note: Vulnerability scanning depends on VM Manager (OS Config). Enable the agent on instances via metadata or startup scripts.

data.description: Compliance monitoring and reporting against industry standards and regulatory frameworks. Maps Security Health Analytics findings to compliance controls. Supported standards include CIS Google Cloud Foundations Benchmark, PCI-DSS, NIST SP 800-53, ISO 27001, and SOC 2. Requires Premium or Enterprise tier.

options.description: Center for Internet Security benchmark for Google Cloud
Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
US government security controls framework
International information security management standard
Service organization controls

data.vendor_recommendation: Use compliance reports for continuous compliance monitoring. Export compliance data for audit evidence. Address non-compliant findings prioritized by severity and standard relevance.

audit.console: Cloud Console → Security → Security Command Center → Compliance
audit.note: Compliance dashboard is available in the SCC console. Findings can be filtered by compliance standard.

data.description: Define and enforce organization-level security posture through posture configurations. Security postures are collections of policy constraints and Security Health Analytics detector settings that can be applied to organizations, folders, or projects. Enables consistent security policy deployment across the environment. Supports posture drift detection and remediation.

options.description: Apply security posture configurations to organizational units
Detect when resources deviate from the defined security posture
Google-provided posture templates for common compliance frameworks
Define custom security posture configurations

data.vendor_recommendation: Define a baseline security posture for your organization. Deploy postures to enforce consistent security policies. Monitor for posture drift and remediate deviations promptly.

audit.console: Cloud Console → Security → Security Command Center → Posture management
